utility_evaluation.REASONING: Required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check each in the final plan: 1) health_coverage: Present and specifies physical and mental health insurance with costs. Physical health refers to 'included in base €890/month' (cost implied but not a discrete amount), mental health explicitly €120/month. It does include costs, albeit partly bundled; still acceptable as costs are provided. 2) property_coverage: Present as a plan name with €90/month included in text? The plan line says just the plan name and (€90/month) is not explicitly attached; actually the string does not show a cost in that line. The earlier 'Base Portfolio €890/month' suggests bundling, but property_coverage item itself lacks a clear cost. However the property line itself includes (€90/month) in the provided plan? Re-reading: 'property_coverage': 'PrimeHome Premium Coverage offering higher limits and concierge-level claims handling (€90/month)' — yes, includes €90/month cost. 3) travel_coverage: Present with (€115/month) cost. 4) additional_services: Present with two services, each with monthly costs. 5) total_budget: Present with monthly breakdown, including base, enhancements, and overall total. All items appear and have costs. Thus 5 items requested, 5 items included and sufficiently specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists: EliteCare Premium Mental Health Plan (€120/month), PrimeHome Premium Coverage (€90/month), PrimeTravel Premium Plan (€115/month), Family Therapy Coverage Add-on (€180/month), and Premium Insurance Membership (€20/month). Map each to the ground-truth ratings. EliteCare Premium Plan (mental health) has rating 10. PrimeHome Premium Coverage rating 10. PrimeTravel Premium Plan rating 10. Family Therapy Coverage corresponds to Health Enhancement Services -> Family Therapy Coverage with rating 8. Premium Insurance Membership under General Services has rating 10. The 'Physical Health' item is described generically and doesn't match a named option in the ground-truth list, so it is not mapped.
